greeting.ai_message: Good morning, Sowmya. 3 signals need your attention before your capital markets committee meeting. The most urgent: PNFED 2024-A collateral delinquencies crossed the 1.80% trigger threshold. If this trend holds for one more reporting period, it activates early amortization. Want me to walk you through them?

signal_1_abs.ai_message:
PNFED 2024-A. The collateral pool's 60+ day delinquency rate moved from 1.36% to 1.78% over the latest reporting period. The deal's early amortization trigger is set at 1.80% sustained over two consecutive periods.

I cross-referenced Snowflake loan-level data with Salesforce Data Cloud origination attributes: the delinquency concentration is in loans originated through three specific dealer partners in Q3 2024, which carry a 4.2% delinquency rate versus 1.1% for the rest of the pool. Bloomberg surveillance shows ABS spreads for comparable prime auto deals widened 8 bps this month, signaling the market is already pricing in sector-level credit deterioration.

Current trajectory projects breach by August 2026.

turn_2_dealer_breakdown.ai_response:
The delinquency concentration maps to three dealer partners.

• Dealer A (Metro Auto Group): 847 loans in the 2024-A pool, 4.7% delinquency rate, financial health score declined from 72 to 58 in the last six months.
• Dealer B (National Auto Finance): 612 loans, 3.9% delinquency rate, recently flagged by the credit monitoring feed for declining floor plan performance.
• Dealer C (Premier Motors Network): 534 loans, 3.8% delinquency rate, stable financial health but geographic concentration in regions with rising unemployment.

These three dealers represent 22% of the 2024-A pool by balance but 61% of the delinquency volume. The friction point: origination channel quality is degrading the securitization collateral, and it is not visible in aggregate portfolio metrics until it crosses a trigger.
suggested_chips: What should we do about these dealers?
Are these dealers in our pipeline for 2025-B?
What happens if we breach the trigger?
Next signal

turn_3_trigger_scenarios.user_query: What happens if we breach the trigger?
turn_3_trigger_scenarios.ai_response:
I modeled three scenarios against the PNFED 2024-A deal structure.

• Scenario A (current trajectory): delinquency reaches 2.04% by August 2026, breaching the trigger for two consecutive periods. Early amortization activates, redirecting all excess spread to principal paydown. $142M reinvestment capacity locked. 2025-B issuance delayed because rating agencies require a seasoning period.

• Scenario B (dealer remediation, RECOMMENDED): tighten origination criteria for the three flagged dealers and accelerate loss mitigation on 47 loans currently 60-89 DPD. Projected delinquency stabilizes at 1.71%, below the trigger. Cost: ~$2.1M accelerated charge-offs this quarter, absorbed in existing provision. Preserves $142M reinvestment capacity and the September 2025-B timeline.

• Scenario C (portfolio substitution): replace highest-risk dealer-originated loans with performing collateral from the retained portfolio. Structural option under the deal documents; requires trustee approval and rating agency notification. Timeline: 6-8 weeks.
suggested_chips: Execute Scenario B
Show me the hedge effectiveness signal
What does this mean for our capital ratio?
Draft a trustee notification for Scenario C

turn_4_hedge_anomaly.user_query: Show me the hedge effectiveness signal
turn_4_hedge_anomaly.ai_response:
Your interest rate swap portfolio shows an anomaly. Three of your twelve active swap positions are driving the hedge effectiveness ratio from 96.2% down to 89.7%.

The common factor: all three are 5-year swaps entered in Q1 2025 when the yield curve was flatter. Since March 2026, the curve has steepened 47 bps in the 2-to-10-year spread, creating a basis mismatch between the swap terms and the underlying fixed-rate securities they hedge.

Individually, these three positions show effectiveness ratios of 78.4%, 81.2%, and 83.6%. FASB ASC 815 requires 80% minimum for hedge accounting. The 78.4% position already fails. If the curve steepens another 15 bps, the 81.2% position fails as well. Losing hedge accounting on the at-risk positions would require $4.8M unrealized losses to flow through earnings rather than other comprehensive income.
suggested_chips: What are my options to fix the hedge?
Model the P&L impact if we lose hedge accounting
Show me the dealer concentration signal
What does this mean for our capital ratio?

ui_components_to_render.title: Yield Curve: Q1 2025 Entry vs. Current

turn_5_capital_impact.user_query: What does this mean for our capital ratio?
turn_5_capital_impact.ai_response:
I ran a consolidated capital impact model across all three signals.

Current regulatory capital ratio: 10.32%.

• Do nothing: if the ABS trigger breaches and you lose hedge accounting on two swap positions, combined impact is a 38 bps reduction to 9.94%. Still well-capitalized, but the buffer above the 7% threshold narrows from $1.04B to $890M. Rating agencies will factor the early amortization event into their surveillance review, which could lead to a negative outlook on outstanding tranches.

• Remediate (Scenario B + restructure the three at-risk swaps): capital ratio impact is limited to 8 bps, holding at 10.24%. The $2.1M in accelerated charge-offs is absorbed within existing provision. Swap restructure costs ~$340K in breakage fees but preserves hedge accounting.
suggested_chips: Execute the remediation path
Generate a board risk summary
Prepare the S&P surveillance package
Model additional rate scenarios

ui_components_to_render.title: Capital Ratio Waterfall — Current to Scenario Outcome

hedge_fix_options.user_query: What are my options to fix the hedge?
hedge_fix_options.ai_response: Three options. (1) Restructure the three at-risk positions — close them and re-enter with terms aligned to the current yield curve. Breakage cost ~$340K, preserves hedge accounting. (2) Layer new offsetting positions on top — cheaper upfront but increases gross notional and adds counterparty exposure. (3) De-designate the failing position and accept the $4.8M earnings impact in the quarter. Option 1 is the cleanest path and is included in the remediation action set.
suggested_chips: Execute the remediation path

The remediation path avoids this entirely.
